---

## Phase 2 — Authentication

This application uses a **simple HTML form**, not Azure AD / Cognito. The `auth.helper.ts` scaffold is present (framework requirement) but is **not invoked**.

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| Type | HTML form (`/login`) |
| Username selector | `#username` |
| Password selector | `#password` |
| Submit selector | `button[type="submit"]` |
| Success signal | `.flash.success` → `"You logged into a secure area"` + redirect `/secure` |
| Error signal | `.flash.error` → `"Your username is invalid"` |
| Logout signal | `.flash.success` → `"You logged out of the secure area"` + redirect `/login` |

**Login flow:**
```
page.goto('/login')
→ fill #username
→ fill #password
→ click button[type="submit"]
→ expect URL /secure + .flash.success
```

---

## Phase 3 — Design Source of Truth

No Figma URL provided in prompt or `.env`. No `FIGMA_FILE_URL` / `FIGMA_FILE_KEY` present. **No design-parity test cases generated.** Visual baseline deferred.

---

## Phase 4 — Business Rules (from `project-memory.md` + CSV)

| ID | Rule |
|---|---|
| BR-001 | Valid credentials → redirect `/secure` + `.flash.success` containing `"You logged into a secure area"` |
| BR-002 | Invalid credentials → stay `/login` + `.flash.error` containing `"Your username is invalid"` |
| BR-003 | Logout → redirect `/login` + `.flash.success` containing `"You logged out of the secure area"` |
| BR-004 | `/download_secure` unauthenticated → redirect to `/login` |
| BR-005 | Dynamic loading pages → `"Hello World!"` shown after Start clicked |
| BR-006 | JS Alert/Confirm/Prompt → `#result` updated with correct confirmation text |

---

## 🤝 Handoff to Test Planner

The **Test Planner** should now:

1. Use `docs/actualtestcases.csv` (40 rows) as the authoritative source — all rows must map to a spec.
2. Generate spec files per the grouping above, one test at a time (rule §6).
3. `tests/login.spec.ts` already covers TC-DEMO-001 and TC-DEMO-002 — **do not regenerate those**.
4. For tests requiring login (TC-008, TC-040), use `beforeAll` + `storageState` for session reuse (no re-auth per test).
5. No Azure AD / `auth.helper.ts` invocation needed.
6. Every test must reference its `Test Case No.` from the CSV (traceability rule §7).
7. Failures → classify as application issue (`docs/issues.csv`) or test issue (`specs/test-analysis.md`).
